test_cases.title: First page response includes valid next_cursor and null prev_cursor
preconditions: At least two pages of data exist for the target list endpoint
No cursor parameter is included in the request
steps: Send a GET request to a list endpoint (e.g., /items) without any cursor parameter
Inspect the response envelope for 'next_cursor' and 'prev_cursor' fields
test_cases.expected_result: Response returns HTTP 200, the envelope contains a non-null 'next_cursor' value and a 'prev_cursor' that is null or absent, and the data array is non-empty.

test_cases.title: Using next_cursor retrieves the subsequent page of results
preconditions: At least two pages of data exist for the target list endpoint
A valid 'next_cursor' value has been obtained from a previous list response
steps: Send a GET request to a list endpoint without any cursor parameter and record the first page's data and 'next_cursor' value
Send a second GET request to the same endpoint with the query parameter cursor=<next_cursor_value>
Compare the data items in the second response against the first response
test_cases.expected_result: Response returns HTTP 200, the second page contains a distinct, non-overlapping set of items compared to the first page, and the response envelope includes 'next_cursor' and 'prev_cursor' fields.

test_cases.title: Last page response contains null or absent next_cursor
preconditions: A finite, known dataset exists so that the last page can be reached
Cursor values have been followed sequentially to reach the last page
steps: Send a GET request to the list endpoint without a cursor parameter
Repeatedly follow 'next_cursor' values by issuing new requests until 'next_cursor' is null or absent in the response
test_cases.expected_result: The final response returns HTTP 200, 'next_cursor' is null or absent in the envelope, and the data array contains only the remaining items with no duplication from prior pages.

test_cases.title: Using prev_cursor navigates back to the previous page
preconditions: At least two pages of data exist
A valid 'prev_cursor' value has been obtained from the second or later page response
steps: Send a GET request without a cursor to obtain the first page; record its data items
Send a GET request using the 'next_cursor' from step 1 to obtain the second page; record its 'prev_cursor' value
Send a GET request using cursor=<prev_cursor_value> from the second page
Compare the returned items against the first page items recorded in step 1
test_cases.expected_result: Response returns HTTP 200 and the items returned match those from the first page, confirming backward navigation is functional.

test_cases.title: Request with an invalid or tampered cursor value returns an appropriate error
steps: Send a GET request to the list endpoint with an invalid cursor value (e.g., cursor=INVALID_CURSOR_XYZ)
Inspect the HTTP response status code and body
test_cases.expected_result: Response returns an HTTP 4xx error (e.g., 400 Bad Request) and the body contains an error message indicating the cursor is invalid; no data items are returned.

test_cases.title: Expired or already-consumed cursor returns an appropriate error or empty result
preconditions: A cursor value has been obtained that is no longer valid (e.g., has expired per system TTL policy or been explicitly invalidated)
steps: Obtain a cursor value from a previous list response
Wait for the cursor to expire or simulate expiry as applicable
Send a GET request to the list endpoint using the expired cursor value
Inspect the HTTP response status code and body
test_cases.expected_result: Response returns an HTTP 4xx error (e.g., 400 or 410 Gone) or a clearly documented fallback behavior such as restarting pagination; the system does not silently return incorrect or duplicate data.

assumptions: Cursor values are passed as a query parameter (e.g., ?cursor=<value>); the exact parameter name is not specified in the requirement.
The requirement applies to all list endpoints uniformly; individual endpoint-level exceptions are not accounted for.
A 'response envelope' is a top-level JSON object wrapping the data array alongside metadata fields including 'next_cursor' and 'prev_cursor'.
Cursors may have an expiration or invalidation policy; the exact policy is not stated in the requirement.
Page size (limit) is a separate concern and may be controlled by another mechanism not described in this requirement.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) the exact query parameter name for submitting a cursor to the server; (2) whether 'prev_cursor' is null or absent on the first page — both are tested as equivalent; (3) the behavior when an expired or previously used cursor is submitted, which is a significant gap; (4) whether cursor values are opaque tokens or structured values, which affects security testing for cursor forgery/tampering; (5) no page size or 'limit' parameter behavior is described, leaving boundary testing for result counts incomplete; (6) there is no mention of what 'next_cursor' and 'prev_cursor' should equal on a single-page result set (both null?), which is an ambiguity requiring clarification.
